data.title: K Wave Media Sells Remaining 88 Bitcoin to Repay $6 Million Debt
data.description:
K Wave Media (KWM), a South Korea-listed company, has sold its remaining 88 BTC to repay $6 million in debt and now holds zero bitcoin.

According to Odaily, BitcoinTreasuries.NET said in a post on X that KWM disposed of all of its remaining bitcoin holdings as part of the debt repayment.

data.title: Lenders to Clearlake-Backed Tungsten Sign Pact After Debt Prices Slide
data.description: Lenders to Tungsten Automation have signed a pact agreeing to work together on a potential debt restructuring after a steep slide in the software company’s debt prices, according to Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Trump’s Nuclear Watchdog to Ditch 50-Year-Old Radiation Guidance
data.description: The US Nuclear Regulatory Commission plans to scrap guidance on radiation exposure for power plant workers that’s been in place since the mid-1970s, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: South Korean Banks Expand FX Trading Teams Ahead of Near 24-Hour Won Trading
data.description:
Major South Korean banks are expanding their foreign-exchange trading teams and strengthening operations in Seoul and London ahead of the launch of a near 24-hour won FX market. According to Jin10, the staffing and system upgrades are intended to support extended trading hours and improve market liquidity.

Woori Bank has assigned eight additional employees to its London office, covering roles in foreign exchange, derivatives, and bond trading. Hana Bank has added traders in Seoul and introduced a three-shift rotation to provide round-the-clock coverage.

Shinhan Bank and KB Kookmin Bank have also increased staffing and upgraded systems as part of the same preparation.

The changes are linked to the introduction of a near 24-hour won foreign-exchange trading framework starting July 6, aimed at boosting liquidity and supporting the internationalization of South Korea’s financial markets.

data.title: Nuclear Startup Valar Atomics Generates Power From Advanced Reactor in US First
data.description: Valar Atomics Inc., a California-based nuclear startup, generated electricity from an advanced reactor to run an Nvidia Corp. AI chip, Bloomberg reported. While the output was only a trickle, the company said it marked the first time a next-generation reactor has produced power in the US.

data.title: Strategy’s MSTR Premium to Bitcoin Holdings Falls Below 1, BitcoinTreasuries.NET Says
data.description:
BitcoinTreasuries.NET said Bloomberg reported that investors are no longer willing to pay the high premium they previously paid for Strategy’s MSTR as Bitcoin has declined. According to Odaily, BitcoinTreasuries.NET wrote on X that Strategy’s premium relative to its Bitcoin holdings had once reached 200% but has now fallen below 1.

The post said the drop indicates the trade is breaking down.

data.title: Brent Crude Falls About 40% From March Peak to Around $72.25
data.description: Brent crude oil fell roughly 40% from its March peak near $120 to around $72.25 on Wednesday. According to NS3.AI, prices returned to a pre-war support base as traders shifted their attention back to supply and demand dynamics and the broader economic outlook.

data.title: Eric Balchunas Says U.S. Treasury Will Use SPYM as Default Fund for Trump Account Investments
data.description:
Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas said the U.S. Treasury will use SPYM as the default fund for Trump Account investments.

According to NS3.AI, Balchunas also said U.S. government fund inflows may expand in the coming years.

data.title: Solana Launches Onchain Governance With 15% Stake Threshold for Votes
data.description:
The Solana Foundation said on Wednesday that onchain governance is now live, introducing a proposal and voting process tied to stake-weighted participation.

According to NS3.AI, proposals must first receive support from at least 15% of cluster stake before they can proceed to a vote.

Under the new system, validators with at least 100,000 SOL delegated to them can open Solana Governance Proposals. Delegators are also able to override a validator’s vote, or a validator’s decision not to vote, using their own stake weight.

The governance mechanism uses Merkle proof to verify stake-weighted votes.

data.title: Chuck E. Cheese CEO Cites Nearly 40% Growth in Birthday Celebrations
data.description:
Chuck E. Cheese CEO Scott Drake said the company’s $99 birthday party offering has helped drive nearly 40% growth in birthday celebrations, according to Bloomberg.

Drake said the company is focused on connecting with customers through family-friendly, clean and safe entertainment options. He spoke with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close.”

data.title: Peer-Reviewed Paper on Bitcoin Price Power Law Published in Elsevier Journal
data.description:
Bitcoin News said on X that a paper titled “A Mechanistic Derivation of the Bitcoin Price Power Law: Network Adoption Dynamics and Generalised Metcalfe Scaling” has been formally published in Elsevier’s journal Nonlinear Science after peer review.

According to Odaily, the paper was written by @Giovann35084111 and @moneyordebt.

The paper argues that Bitcoin’s long-term price power law can be derived from network adoption dynamics and generalized Metcalfe scaling.

It also states that this provides a theoretical basis for one of Bitcoin’s most disputed valuation models.

data.title: Explosive-Laden Drone Hits Iranian Kurdish Opposition Camp Near Erbil, Iraqi Security Sources Say
data.description:
An explosive-laden drone struck a camp belonging to an Iranian Kurdish opposition group in an area east of Erbil, Iraqi security sources said.

According to Jin10, the report cited Iraqi security sources as saying the drone was carrying explosives and hit the camp located east of Erbil in Iraq.

data.title: One Leveraged ETF Is Starting to Move SK Hynix Shares
data.description: A Hong Kong leveraged fund tied to SK Hynix has grown so large that it is beginning to move the stock it was built to track, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Eric Balchunas Says He Was Surprised VOO Was Not Included on a List
data.description:
Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas said in a post on X that he was somewhat surprised that VOO did not appear on a list.

According to Odaily, Balchunas wrote that the phrase “SPYM & Chill” does not have the same effect, but he would try to make it work.

He added that SPYM’s fee is 2 basis points and said that even the government pays attention to costs.

data.title: DDC Enterprise Approves $10 Million Common Stock Buyback Plan, BitcoinTreasuries.NET Says
data.description: DDC Enterprise’s board has approved a $10 million common stock buyback plan, according to a post by BitcoinTreasuries.NET on X. According to Odaily, BitcoinTreasuries.NET said the company’s mNAV has fallen to 0.3 times.

data.title: Japan Targets $13 Billion Global Anime Merchandise Market
data.description: Anime has become a global entertainment powerhouse, drawing billions of dollars of investment and fans worldwide. Companies including Marubeni are targeting the fast-growing $13 billion anime merchandise market, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: South Korean Banks Expand Trading Floors Ahead of 24-Hour Won Market
data.description: South Korea’s biggest lenders are expanding trading desks in London and Seoul to prepare for the won’s round-the-clock trading starting next week, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: New York Power Prices Surge as Extreme Heat Strains US Grids
data.description: Power prices from New York to Virginia are surging as sweltering heat threatens to overwhelm US grids and disrupt travel for the Independence Day holiday, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Apple to Launch New iPad Pro and Redesigned Entry-Level MacBook Pro, Report Says
data.description:
Apple is preparing to launch a new iPad Pro and a redesigned entry-level MacBook Pro, according to a market report.

According to Jin10, the products include a new iPad Pro model and an updated version of the entry-level MacBook Pro with a redesigned form factor.

The report did not provide a specific launch date or additional product details.

data.title: STBL Launches USST Stablecoin on Stellar Backed by Tokenized Treasury Asset USDY
data.description:
STBL launched its institutional stablecoin, USST, on the Stellar network on July 1, 2026, backed by real-world assets with tokenized Treasury asset USDY as the initial minting collateral. According to Odaily, USST is built on STBL’s Stablecoin 2.0 infrastructure.

STBL said market participants can deposit eligible real-world assets, including tokenized Treasuries or money market funds, to mint USST. The company said the stablecoin supports settlement, collateral mobility, and cross-border payments.

STBL also said it plans to add Franklin Templeton’s BENJI as a second collateral option, but has not set a launch date for BENJI collateral integration. STBL did not disclose the initial minting size of USST on Stellar.
